revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24744680692|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hayatani Shrine]][[en:Hayatani Shrine]][[ja:速谷神社]][[no:Hayatani-helligdommen]][[simple:Hayatani Shrine]][[tok:tomo sewi Kajatani]] {{Expand Japanese|速谷神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hayatani Shrine | native_name = 速谷神社 | image = Hayatani Shrine 20121124-02.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hayatani Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.359164|132.308749}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | website = {{Official website|https://hayatanijinja.jp/index.html}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hayatani Shrine'''|速谷神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Aki Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Beppyo shrine|lt=Beppyo Shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|mr|बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|simple|Beppyo shrine|zh|別表神社|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} * {{ill|Myojin Taisha|lt=Myōjin Taisha|en|Myojin Taisha|fr|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917533|ltq=Q134917533|qid=Q134917533|1=Ni-no-Miya}} ** P1001: {{ill|Aki Province|lt=Aki Province|az|Aki vilayəti|cs|Provincie Aki|de|Provinz Aki|en|Aki Province|es|Provincia de Aki|fa|ولایت آکی|fr|Aki (province)|gl|Provincia de Aki|id|Provinsi Aki|it|Aki (provincia)|ja|安芸国|ko|아키국|lt|Akio žemė|nl|Aki (provincie)|pt|Província de Aki|ru|Аки (провинция)|simple|Aki Province|sk|Aki (provincia)|sr|Провинција Аки|tr|Aki (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Акі|zh_min_nan|Aki-kok|zh|安藝國|qid=Q965029|1=Aki Province}} * {{ill|Q135009132|ltq=Q135009132|qid=Q135009132|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings}} * {{ill|Q135160338|ltq=Q135160338|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Aki Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Aki Province|ja|安芸国の式内社一覧|simple|List of Shikinaisha in Aki Province|qid=Q11451199|1=List of Shikinaisha in Aki Province}} ** Series ordinal: 1 ** P155: Hiroshima|tr|Hatsukaichi|tt|Хацукаити|tum|Hatsukaichi|uk|Хацукаїті|uz|Hatsukaichi|vi|Hatsukaichi, Hiroshima|war|Hatsukaichi, ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 258443394 == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 9240005006786 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00412312 == Commons category (P373) == * Hayatani Shrine == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '広島県廿日市市上平良308-1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hayatani Shrine}} [[EN:Hayatani Shrine]] [[JA:速谷神社]] [[NO:Hayatani-helligdommen]] [[SIMPLE:Hayatani Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q11639721}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Hayatani Shrine |画像 = [[File:Hayatani-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (with the Aki-no-Kuni-no-Miyatsuko monument on the left) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|qid=Q617375|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hatsukaichi|Q736897|qid=Q736897|1=Hatsukaichi}} Kamihiraira 308-1 |位置 = {{Coord|34|21|33.5|N|132|18|30.1|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Akihayatamao-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
{{ill|lt=Aki Province|Q965029|qid=Q965029|1=Aki Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokuhei Chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Beppyō Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 12|Q2920|qid=Q2920|1=October 12}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Hiroshima|label=Hayatani Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|33.5|N|132|18|30.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|21|33.5|N|132|18|30.1|E}}}} }} [[ファイル:Hayatani Shrine 1941.jpg|thumb|Prewar haiden]] '''Hayatani Shrine''' (はやたにじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamihiraira, {{ill|lt=Hatsukaichi|Q736897|qid=Q736897|1=Hatsukaichi}}, {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|qid=Q617375|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and the {{ill|lt=Aki Province|Q965029|qid=Q965029|1=Aki Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}. Under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokuhei Chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and is now a {{ill|lt=Beppyō Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} of the {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q908077|qid=Q908077|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. === Overview === A shrine enshrining the ancestral deity of the founding of {{ill|lt=Aki Province|Q965029|qid=Q965029|1=Aki Province}}, and the general tutelary shrine of Aki Province. With a history of over 1,800 years, it was once considered of higher rank than {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|qid=Q191763|1=Itsukushima Shrine}}. It is known nationwide as a shrine for prayers for {{ill|lt=traffic safety|Q1147899|qid=Q1147899|1=Road safety}}, and many worshippers come from afar with the saying, “If you buy a car, go to Hayatani-san.” It is also famous for buses and trains of {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Electric Railway|Q1196530|qid=Q1196530|1=Hiroshima Electric Railway}} receiving purification rites. Along with {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|qid=Q191763|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Take Shrine|Q11430665|qid=Q11430665|1=Take Shrine}}, it is one of the “Three Great Shrines of Aki Province.” Many traditions remain of travelers on the {{ill|lt=San'yōdō|Q1376722|qid=Q1376722|1=San'yōdō}} praying for safety on long journeys, and it is still revered as the “guardian deity of traffic safety” protecting the San'yōdō. === Enshrined deity === * Akihayatamao-no-Mikoto *: Said to be the fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-Yuzuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, one of the 32 deities accompanying the {{ill|lt=Tenson kōrin|Q1084733|qid=Q1084733|1=Tenson kōrin}}.From the official website. Listed in the '{{ill|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko hongi|Q133833194|qid=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}' as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Aki no miyatsuko|1=UNKNOWN}}."Place Names of Hiroshima Prefecture," entry for Hayatani Shrine. === History === Although the year of enshrinement is unclear, it is an ancient shrine with more than 1,800 years since founding, and is considered the oldest shrine in {{ill|lt=Aki Province|Q965029|qid=Q965029|1=Aki Province}}.{{Cite web |url=https://hayatanijinja.jp/img/taisainiyosete.pdf |title=From “The Oldest Shrine Transmitting the Earliest Aki” (Naoki Shintani) |access-date=2025-08-10}} In the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Saga|Q316654|qid=Q316654|1=Emperor Saga}}, in {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|qid=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=811|Q29946|qid=Q29946|1=811}}), it was listed as a {{ill|lt=Myōjin shrine|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|lt=myōjin|1=Myōjin Taisha}} as recorded in the official history '{{ill|lt=Nihon Kōki|Q1077029|qid=Q1077029|1=Nihon Kōki}}'; this is the oldest record concerning a shrine in Hiroshima Prefecture.{{Cite web|title=“Chronology of Hiroshima Prefecture History,” from ancient times|url=https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/soshiki_file/monjokan/nenpyou/nenpyou-kodai.pdf|access-date=2025-09-01}} In {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 1 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seiwa|Q349243|qid=Q349243|1=Emperor Seiwa}}, its {{ill|lt=court rank for deities|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was raised from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade; in year 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|qid=Q30347|1=867}}), it was further advanced to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade. In {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|qid=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=905|Q24239|qid=Q24239|1=905}}), during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Daigo|Q329753|qid=Q329753|1=Emperor Daigo}}, the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' records “Saeki District, Aki Province: Hayatani Shrine, Myōjin Taisha, Tsukinami, Niiname,” listing it among the imperial-offering {{ill|lt=Myōjin Taisha|Q9610964|qid=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}} shrines. As a shrine for protection of the state, it received offerings from the {{ill|lt=Jingikan|Q2264720|qid=Q2264720|1=Department of Divinities}} at the three annual rites: {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|qid=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}}, {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|qid=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}}, and {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. In this way it was deeply revered by the court from ancient times, and in antiquity held the highest shrine rank not only in Aki and Bingo Provinces but among the eight provinces of the {{ill|lt=San'yōdō|Q1376722|qid=Q1376722|1=San'yōdō}}. In {{ill|lt=Jōhei|Q1324874|qid=Q1324874|lt=Jōhei|1=Jōhei}} 5 ({{ill|lt=935|Q24867|qid=Q24867|1=935}}) during the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suzaku|Q361019|qid=Q361019|1=Emperor Suzaku}}, in the {{ill|lt=Jōhei-Tengyō disturbances#Fujiwara no Sumitomo rebellion|Q2975322|qid=Q2975322|lt=Fujiwara no Sumitomo rebellion|1=Tengyō no Ran}}, the court prayed for pacification at thirteen shrines nationwide including Hayatani Shrine; when the disturbance was soon subdued, its {{ill|lt=deity rank|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} was raised to Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade. It is said to have originally been the {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|qid=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}} of Aki Province, but as {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|qid=Q191763|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} came to be patronized by the {{ill|lt=Taira clan|Q1079102|qid=Q1079102|1=Taira clan}}, it came to be called the Aki Province Ninomiya. From the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}, successive lords including Fujiwara Chikazane and later castellans of {{ill|lt=Sakurao Castle|Q11537973|qid=Q11537973|1=Sakurao Castle}} in {{ill|lt=Aki Province|Q965029|qid=Q965029|1=Aki Province}}, Sengoku daimyō such as {{ill|lt=Ōuchi Yoshitaka|Q1065163|qid=Q1065163|1=Ōuchi Yoshitaka}} and {{ill|lt=Mōri Motonari|Q1156545|qid=Q1156545|1=Mōri Motonari}}, and later Hiroshima domain lords beginning with {{ill|lt=Asano Mitsuakira|Q11557318|qid=Q11557318|1=Asano Mitsuakira}} all deeply revered Hayatani Shrine, donating sacred treasures and shrine lands, and carrying out construction and repairs of the shrine buildings. In {{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|qid=Q2173|1=1924}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kokuhei Chūsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 21 ({{ill|lt=1946|Q18610|qid=Q18610|1=1946}}), with abolition of the government system, it became a {{ill|lt=Beppyō Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} under the Association of Shinto Shrines. === Court ranks === * {{ill|lt=Kōnin|Q1373070|qid=Q1373070|1=Kōnin (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=811|Q29946|qid=Q29946|1=811}}), {{ill|lt=July 17|Q2729|qid=Q2729|1=July 17}}, listed as Myōjin ('Nihon Kōki') – written as “Hayatani no Kami.” * {{ill|lt=Ten'an|Q1075611|qid=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}), {{ill|lt=January 27|Q2284|qid=Q2284|1=January 27}}, from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade ('Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku') – written as “Hayatani no Kami.” * {{ill|lt=Jōgan|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} 9 ({{ill|lt=867|Q30347|qid=Q30347|1=867}}), {{ill|lt=October 13|Q2925|qid=Q2925|1=October 13}}, Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade ('Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku') – written as “Hayatani no Kami.” === Precincts === ファイル:1拝殿（別の角度より）.jpg|リンク=|代替文=|Haiden (from another angle) ファイル:2儀式殿.jpg|リンク=|代替文=|Ritual Hall ファイル:3楼門.jpg|リンク=|代替文=|Rōmon Gate ファイル:4参道.jpg|リンク=|代替文=|Approach ファイル:5阿岐国造碑.jpg|リンク=|代替文=|Aki no miyatsuko monument ファイル:6一の鳥居.jpg|リンク=|代替文=|First torii ファイル:速谷神社の社務所.jpg|Shrine office ファイル:車祓所.jpg|Vehicle purification area ファイル:速谷神社 神楽殿.jpg|Kagura hall === Affiliated and minor shrines === * Iwaki Shrine - Enshrined deity: Iwaki-Okina-no-Kami. Said to be a local deity from before the main shrine’s enshrinement, and there is a theory identifying it with “Ishiki Myōjin” recorded in the 'Aki no Kuni Jinmyōchō.' * Inari Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}. === Rituals ===
Hayatani Shrine Annual Ritual List
* Every month ** {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|qid=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} (12th day of each month, January–December) ** Gesshu-sai (1st day of each month, February–December) * January ** {{ill|lt=Saitan-sai|Q11546824|qid=Q11546824|1=Saitan-sai}} (January 1) ** Enmei-sai (January 2) ** {{ill|lt=Genshi-sai|Q11387821|qid=Q11387821|1=Genshi-sai}} (January 3) ** Grand Festival for Traffic Safety (January 7) ** Old sacred amulet burning festival (January 14) * February ** Setsubun Festival Yudate rite (February 3) ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kigen-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} (February 11) ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tenchō-sai|1=UNKNOWN}} (February 23) * March ** {{ill|lt=Kinen-sai|Q118906710|qid=Q118906710|1=Kinensai}} (March 17) * April ** Ōka-sai (Cherry Blossom Festival) (April 10) ** Shōwa Festival (April 29) * June ** Iwaki Go-Are Festival (June 5) ** {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|qid=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} rite (June 30) * July ** Summer Festival (July 10) * August ** Memorial Festival for Atomic Bomb Victims (August 6) *10月 ** {{ill|lt=Reisai|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} Aki Festival (October 12) * November ** {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} (November 23) * December ** {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|qid=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} rite (December 31)
=== Cultural properties === === Hiroshima Prefecture-designated Important Cultural Properties === * Paper manuscripts in ink: Daiganji Sonkai documents * Wooden {{ill|lt=komainu|Q20155846|qid=Q20155846|1=Komainu}} === Site information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|Q617375|qid=Q617375|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hatsukaichi|Q736897|qid=Q736897|1=Hatsukaichi}} Kamihiraira 308-1 ; Access * Rail ** {{ill|lt=Hiroshima Electric Railway|Q1196530|qid=Q1196530|1=Hiroshima Electric Railway}}{{ill|lt=Hiroden Miyajima Line|Q5371364|qid=Q5371364|lt=Miyajima Line|1=Hiroden Miyajima Line}} from {{ill|lt=Hatsukaichi-shiyakusho-mae (Hira) Station|Q5681851|qid=Q5681851|1=Hatsukaichi-shiyakusho-mae Station}} *** About 30 minutes on foot *** Bus **** Get off at “Hayatani Jinja” bus stop on Hatsukaichi city loop bus ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hatsukaichi Sakura Bus|1=UNKNOWN}} Hara/Kawasue direction) (1 minute on foot after getting off) **** Get off at “Nakahiraira” bus stop on Hatsukaichi city loop bus (Hatsukaichi Sakura Bus west loop line) (5 minutes on foot after getting off) ** {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|qid=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}}{{ill|lt=Sanyō Main Line|Q1193436|qid=Q1193436|1=San'yō Main Line}} from {{ill|lt=Hatsukaichi Station|Q5358343|qid=Q5358343|1=Hatsukaichi Station}} or {{ill|lt=Miyauchikushido Station|Q5358350|qid=Q5358350|1=Miyauchi-Kushido Station}} *** About 30 minutes on foot *** About 10 minutes by taxi * Private car ** From {{ill|lt=Sanyō Expressway|Q7413195|qid=Q7413195|1=San'yō Expressway}} *** From {{ill|lt=Miyajima Service Area|Q11454095|qid=Q11454095|lt=Miyajima SA|1=Miyajima service area}}{{ill|lt=smart interchange|Q11313284|qid=Q11313284|lt=Smart IC|1=Smart interchange}}: 2 minutes *** From {{ill|lt=Hatsukaichi Interchange|Q888167|qid=Q888167|lt=Hatsukaichi IC|1=Hatsukaichi interchange}}: 5 minutes via {{ill|lt=Nishi-Hiroshima Bypass|Q11628231|qid=Q11628231|1=Nishihiroshima bypass}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=National Route 433|1=UNKNOWN}} === Footnotes === {{reflist}} === Bibliography === * 'Japan Historical Place Names Series: Place Names of Hiroshima Prefecture' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Saeki District, Hayatani Shrine === External links === {{commonscat|Hayatani Shrine}} *
